title: IReactorClient methods accept AbortSignal for cancelling in-flight requests without stopping server-side processing
content: Most `IReactorClient` methods accept an optional `AbortSignal` parameter for cancellation. Use cases: cancel on React component unmount via `useEffect` cleanup with `AbortController`, cancel with timeout via `AbortSignal.timeout(5000)`, and cancel long-running writes where `execute()` waits for `READ_READY`. When aborted, methods throw `AbortError`. Important: aborting only cancels the client-side wait, not the server-side processing -- the underlying reactor job may still complete. Write methods like `execute()` wait for `READ_READY` status; `executeAsync()` returns immediately with a `PENDING` JobInfo, and `waitForJob()` can be used separately for tracking.
description: Aborting cancels the client-side wait only; the underlying reactor job may still complete on the server side.
